context.pr_title: Fix build error with GCC by forcing Clang in CMake on android/aarch64
context.pr_body:
GCC does not recognize Clang-specific warning flags like -Wunreachable-code-break and -Wunreachable-code-return, which are passed by upstream submodules (e.g., ggml). This patch forces CMake to use Clang via command-line arguments, avoiding the need to patch nested submodules.

This resolves compiler errors without modifying submodule source code.

agents.system_prompt: You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README # bitnet.cpp [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) ![version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-1.0-blue) [](https://huggingface.co/microsoft/BitNet-b1.58-2B-4T) Try it out via this [demo](https://bitnet-demo.azurewebsites.net/), or [build and run](https://github.com/microsoft/BitNet?tab=readme-ov-file#build-from-source) it on your own CPU. bitnet.cpp is the official inference framework for 1-bit LLMs (e.g., BitNet b1.58). It offers a suite of optimized kernels, that support **fast** and **lossless** inference of 1.58-bit models on CPU (with NPU and GPU support coming next). The first release of bitnet.cpp is to support inference on CPUs. bitnet.cpp achieves speedups of **1.37x** to **5.07x** on ARM CPUs, with larger models experiencing greater performance gains. Additionally, it reduces energy consumption by **55.4%** to **70.0%**, further boosting overall efficiency. On x86 CPUs, speedups range from **2.37x** to **6.17x** with energy reductions between **71.9%** to **82.2%**. Furthermore, bitnet.cpp can run a 100B BitNet b1.58 model on a single CPU, achieving speeds comparable to human reading (5-7 tokens per second), significantly enhancing the potential for running LLMs on local devices. Please refer to the [technical report](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16144) for more details. >The tested models are dummy setups used in a research context to demonstrate the inference performance of bitnet.cpp. ## Demo A demo of bitnet.cpp running a BitNet b1.58 3B model on Apple M2: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7f46b736-edec-4828-b809-4be780a3e5b1 ## What's New: - 04/14/2025 [BitNet Official 2B Parameter Model on Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/microsoft/BitNet-b1.58-2B-4T) ![NEW](https://img.shields.io/badge/NEW-red) - 02/18/2025 [Bitnet.cpp: Efficient Edge Inference for Ternary LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.11880) - 11/08/2024 [BitNet a4.8: 4-bit Activations for 1-bit LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.04965) - 10/21/2024 [1-bit AI Infra: Part 1.1, Fast and Lossless BitNet b1.58 Inference on CPUs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16144) - 10/17/2024 bitnet.cpp 1.0 released. - 03/21/2024 [The-Era-of-1-bit-LLMs__Training_Tips_Code_FAQ](https://github.com/microsoft/unilm/blob/master/bitnet/The-Era-of-1-bit-LLMs__Training_Tips_Code_FAQ.pdf) - 02/27/2024 [The Era of 1-bit LLMs: All Large Language Models are in 1.58 Bits](https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.17764) - 10/17/2023 [BitNet: Scaling 1-bit Transformers for Large Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.11453) ## Acknowledgements This project is based on the [llama.cpp](https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp) framework. We would like to thank all the authors for their contrib ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods

tool_calls.raw_result:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.14)  # for add_link_options and implicit target directories.
project("bitnet.cpp" C CXX)
include(CheckIncludeFileCXX)

set(CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS ON)

if (NOT XCODE AND NOT MSVC AND NOT CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE)
    set(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE Release CACHE STRING "Build type" FORCE)
    set_property(CACHE CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE PROPERTY STRINGS "Debug" "Release" "MinSizeRel" "RelWithDebInfo")
endif()

set(CMAKE_RUNTIME_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/bin)

# option list
option(BITNET_ARM_TL1    "bitnet.cpp: use tl1 on arm platform"    OFF)
option(BITNET_X86_TL2    "bitnet.cpp: use tl2 on x86 platform"    OFF)


set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED true)
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED true)
set(THREADS_PREFER_PTHREAD_FLAG ON)

# override ggml options
set(GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1    ${BITNET_ARM_TL1})
set(GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2    ${BITNET_X86_TL2})

if (GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1)
    add_compile_definitions(GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1)
endif()
if (GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2)
    add_compile_definitions(GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2)
endif()

if (CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID STREQUAL "GNU" OR CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID STREQUAL "GNU")
    add_compile_options(-fpermissive)
endif()

find_package(Threads REQUIRED)

add_subdirectory(src)
add_subdirectory(3rdparty/llama.cpp)

# install

include(GNUInstallDirs)
include(CMakePackageConfigHelpers)

set(LLAMA_INCLUDE_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}
    CACHE PATH "Location of header files")
set(LLAMA_LIB_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    CACHE PATH "Location of library files")
set(LLAMA_BIN_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}
    CACHE PATH "Location of binary files")
set(LLAMA_BUILD_NUMBER ${BUILD_NUMBER})
set(LLAMA_BUILD_COMMIT ${BUILD_COMMIT})
set(LLAMA_INSTALL_VERSION 0.0.${BUILD_NUMBER})

get_target_property(GGML_DIRECTORY ggml SOURCE_DIR)
get_directory_property(GGML_DIR_DEFINES DIRECTORY ${GGML_DIRECTORY} COMPILE_DEFINITIONS)
get_target_property(GGML_TARGET_DEFINES ggml COMPILE_DEFINITIONS)
set(GGML_TRANSIENT_DEFINES ${GGML_TARGET_DEFINES} ${GGML_DIR_DEFINES})
get_target_property(GGML_LINK_LIBRARIES ggml LINK_LIBRARIES)

get_directory_property(LLAMA_TRANSIENT_DEFINES COMPILE_DEFINITIONS)

write_basic_package_version_file(
        ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/LlamaConfigVersion.cmake
    VERSION ${LLAMA_INSTALL_VERSION}
    COMPATIBILITY SameMajorVersion)

install(FILES ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/LlamaConfig.cmake
              ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/LlamaConfigVersion.cmake
        DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/Llama)

set_target_properties(llama PROPERTIES PUBLIC_HEADER ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/llama.h)
install(TARGETS llama LIBRARY PUBLIC_HEADER)

### File Overview
This is the top-level CMakeLists.txt for the bitnet.cpp project: it sets global CMake variables and options, some compile flags and definitions, configures output/install locations, and adds the main subdirectories (src and 3rdparty/llama.cpp) that do most of the build work.

### Architecture
- Top-level configuration and global settings are applied first (CMake minimum version, project languages, export compile commands, build type defaulting, runtime output dir).
- Two project-specific options (BITNET_ARM_TL1, BITNET_X86_TL2) are defined and propagated into GGML-related defines.
- A small set of global compile settings are applied (C/C++ standard requirements, THREADS_PREFER_PTHREAD_FLAG, GNU-specific -fpermissive).
- The real source targets are added via add_subdirectory(src) and add_subdirectory(3rdparty/llama.cpp) — these subdirectories are likely where target-specific compiler/toolchain configuration would appear.
- Finally, install/package helpers and properties are configured (GNUInstallDirs, CMakePackageConfigHelpers, writing config/version files, install targets and headers).

### Functions & Classes
(Each line below preserves the exact CMake command or variable as it appears in the file, with a short description of its role and importance.)

- `cmake_minimum_required` — sets the minimum CMake version to 3.14 (needed for add_link_options and implicit target directories). Important to ensure expected CMake features are available.

- `project("bitnet.cpp" C CXX)` — declares the project name and enables C and C++ languages. Establishes the overall project language scope.

- `include(CheckIncludeFileCXX)` — includes the CMake module CheckIncludeFileCXX. Likely used later (in subdirectories) to check headers.

- `set(CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS ON)` — enables generation of compile_commands.json which is useful for tooling (linters, language servers).

- `if (NOT XCODE AND NOT MSVC AND NOT CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE) ... set(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE Release CACHE STRING "Build type" FORCE)` — default to `Release` build type when not using Xcode or MSVC and when no user-specified CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE exists; this forces a build type in single-config generators. Important because it forces optimization by default.

- `set(CMAKE_RUNTIME_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/bin)` — directs runtime binaries to a bin folder under the build dir.

- `option(BITNET_ARM_TL1    "bitnet.cpp: use tl1 on arm platform"    OFF)` — user-configurable option to enable ARM-specific TL1 support. Relevant for platform-specific behavior.

- `option(BITNET_X86_TL2    "bitnet.cpp: use tl2 on x86 platform"    OFF)` — user-configurable option to enable x86-specific TL2 support.

- `set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED true)` — requires that a C++ standard is used; enforces standard handling (note: the file does not set a specific CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD value here).

- `set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)` — sets the C standard to C11.

- `set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED true)` — requires that configured C standard be respected.

- `set(THREADS_PREFER_PTHREAD_FLAG ON)` — prefer pthread flag for the Threads package (affects find_package(Threads)).

- `set(GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1    ${BITNET_ARM_TL1})` — propagates BITNET_ARM_TL1 into a GGML-prefixed variable for downstream use.

- `set(GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2    ${BITNET_X86_TL2})` — propagates BITNET_X86_TL2 into a GGML-prefixed variable for downstream use.

- `if (GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1) add_compile_definitions(GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1) endif()` — when enabled, add a preprocessor definition `GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1`. This is how ARM-specific code paths are enabled.

- `if (GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2) add_compile_definitions(GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2) endif()` — similarly adds `GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2` define when enabled.

- `if (CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID STREQUAL "GNU" OR CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID STREQUAL "GNU") add_compile_options(-fpermissive) endif()` — detects GNU compilers via `CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID` or `CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID` and adds `-fpermissive` for compilation. This is the only compiler-id-based conditional logic in this top-level file.

- `find_package(Threads REQUIRED)` — finds threading support; combined with THREADS_PREFER_PTHREAD_FLAG influences pthread usage.

- `add_subdirectory(src)` — includes the src directory CMakeLists (where the main targets are defined). Critical: target-specific compiler flags and possibly toolchain choices may be inside this subdirectory.

- `add_subdirectory(3rdparty/llama.cpp)` — includes the 3rdparty/llama.cpp CMakeLists. Also critical: ggml/llama targets and any compile-time options or link libraries are likely configured here.

- `include(GNUInstallDirs)` — include standard GNU install dir variables.

- `include(CMakePackageConfigHelpers)` — helpers for generating package config/version files.

- `set(LLAMA_INCLUDE_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR} CACHE PATH "Location of header files")` — sets cached install location variables used for packaging.

- `set(LLAMA_LIB_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR} CACHE PATH "Location of library files")` — as above for libraries.

- `set(LLAMA_BIN_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR} CACHE PATH "Location of binary files")` — as above for binaries.

- `set(LLAMA_BUILD_NUMBER ${BUILD_NUMBER})` — passes BUILD_NUMBER into an LLAMA_* variable (BUILD_NUMBER itself presumably comes from the environment or caller).

- `set(LLAMA_BUILD_COMMIT ${BUILD_COMMIT})` — passes BUILD_COMMIT similarly.

- `set(LLAMA_INSTALL_VERSION 0.0.${BUILD_NUMBER})` — composes an install version from BUILD_NUMBER.

- `get_target_property(GGML_DIRECTORY ggml SOURCE_DIR)` — queries the ggml target's SOURCE_DIR. Used to find compile definitions from ggml directory.

- `get_directory_property(GGML_DIR_DEFINES DIRECTORY ${GGML_DIRECTORY} COMPILE_DEFINITIONS)` — fetches directory-level COMPILE_DEFINITIONS from ggml directory.

- `get_target_property(GGML_TARGET_DEFINES ggml COMPILE_DEFINITIONS)` — fetches target-level COMPILE_DEFINITIONS for ggml.

- `set(GGML_TRANSIENT_DEFINES ${GGML_TARGET_DEFINES} ${GGML_DIR_DEFINES})` — aggregates ggml compile definitions into GGML_TRANSIENT_DEFINES (used later when writing package config).

- `get_target_property(GGML_LINK_LIBRARIES ggml LINK_LIBRARIES)` — retrieves link libraries used by ggml.

- `get_directory_property(LLAMA_TRANSIENT_DEFINES COMPILE_DEFINITIONS)` — fetches top-level directory COMPILE_DEFINITIONS for packaging.

- `write_basic_package_version_file(${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/LlamaConfigVersion.cmake VERSION ${LLAMA_INSTALL_VERSION} COMPATIBILITY SameMajorVersion)` — writes a version file for package config.

- `install(FILES ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/LlamaConfig.cmake ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/LlamaConfigVersion.cmake DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/Llama)` — installs generated package config files.

- `set_target_properties(llama PROPERTIES PUBLIC_HEADER ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/llama.h)` — marks public header for target `llama` (exposes header for install).

- `install(TARGETS llama LIBRARY PUBLIC_HEADER)` — installs the `llama` target library and its public headers.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
The agent is looking for where compilers, CMAKE_<LANG>_COMPILER, toolchains, or platform-specific settings (android/aarch64) are chosen or flags are set. Key findings:

- This top-level CMakeLists.txt does NOT set `CMAKE_C_COMPILER` or `CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER`, and it does not reference any toolchain file. There is no direct forcing/overriding of compilers here.
- The only compiler-specific logic present is the conditional check on `CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID` / `CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID` to add `-fpermissive` for GNU compilers.
- Build type is forcibly set to `Release` via CACHE/ FORCE for non-Xcode/MSVC single-config generators; this is a forced setting but not a compiler selection.
- Platform-specific options exist as `option(BITNET_ARM_TL1 ...)` and `option(BITNET_X86_TL2 ...)` and these are propagated to `GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1` / `GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2` and add compile definitions `GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1` / `GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2`. These enable platform-dependent code paths, but do not themselves choose compiler/toolchain.
- Important places to check next (because top-level does not set compiler/toolchain):
  - `add_subdirectory(src)` — check src/CMakeLists.txt for any per-target compile flags, toolchain selection, or CMAKE_<LANG>_COMPILER settings.
  - `add_subdirectory(3rdparty/llama.cpp)` — check 3rdparty/llama.cpp/CMakeLists.txt and ggml target definitions because this file queries ggml target properties (GGML_* variables) and relies on ggml being defined there; ggml may set architecture-specific flags or intrinsics (aarch64/android).
- If you need to force a particular compiler or supply a toolchain file, do so on the CMake command line (e.g., -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=... or -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=... or -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=...), since this top-level file provides no override option for compilers.

Summary: top-level CMakeLists sets global flags and defines, including option-driven compile definitions for ARM/x86 paths and a GNU-specific compile option. It does not set compilers or toolchain files — inspect src/ and 3rdparty/llama.cpp for any target-level or platform-specific compiler/toolchain logic (especially for android/aarch64).

### File Overview
This header declares the BitNet-related public API for ggml's BitNet quantized matrix operations and the per-tensor extra metadata structure. It contains platform-dependent typedefs and conditional declarations for ARM and x86 optimized routines.

### Architecture
- Top-level includes: "ggml.h" and "ggml-backend.h" — this header relies on types and macros defined there (e.g., GGML_API, enum ggml_type).
- Platform selection is done via preprocessor macros:
  - __ARM_NEON controls whether arm_neon.h is included and whether bitnet_float_type is typedef'd to float32_t or float.
  - GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1 and GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2 enable platform-specific optimized function prototypes (different prototypes for ARM TL1 vs x86 TL2).
- All public symbols are exported with GGML_API and wrapped in extern "C" for C++ compatibility.
- The header declares:
  - a per-tensor extra metadata struct bitnet_tensor_extra,
  - API functions for initialization, multiplication capability checks, workspace sizing, task initialization/compute, tensor transformation, type bits query, and thread control,
  - and platform-specific low-level qgemm/preprocessor functions under the two GGML_BITNET_* macros.

### Functions & Classes
- bitnet_float_type — typedef
  - Defined as float32_t when __ARM_NEON is defined (and arm_neon.h is included), otherwise typedef'd to float.
  - Importance: determines the floating point type used for scales and other floating computations in BitNet code. This is a platform-dependent typedef chosen by a compile-time macro (__ARM_NEON).

- struct bitnet_tensor_extra
  - Fields:
    - int lut_scales_size;
    - int BK;
    - int n_tile_num;
    - uint8_t * qweights;
    - bitnet_float_type * scales;
  - Importance: carries extra per-tensor metadata needed by BitNet routines (lookup table scales size, block size BK, number of tiles, pointer to quantized weights, pointer to scales). Crucial for any code that inspects or transforms BitNet-quantized tensors.

- ggml_bitnet_init(void)
  - Purpose: initialize BitNet subsystem (likely allocate global resources, lookup tables, or thread pools).
  - Importance: should be called before other BitNet operations.

- ggml_bitnet_free(void)
  - Purpose: free resources allocated by ggml_bitnet_init.
  - Importance: cleanup counterpart to ggml_bitnet_init.

- ggml_bitnet_can_mul_mat(const struct ggml_tensor * src0, const struct ggml_tensor * src1, const struct ggml_tensor * dst)
  - Purpose: return whether BitNet supports matrix-multiplication for the given tensor types/shapes. The comment notes supported input types (Q4_0/IQ2_XXS/IQ3_XXS) and that BitNet code currently only supports BitNet quantization or GPTQ-like quantization (scales-only, no zeros).
  - Importance: used to select BitNet-friendly code paths; important guard for correctness (the header comment warns that i-quantization gguf models will yield wrong results).

- ggml_bitnet_mul_mat_get_wsize(const struct ggml_tensor * src0, const struct ggml_tensor * src1, const struct ggml_tensor * dst)
  - Purpose: compute required workspace size for a BitNet matrix multiplication between the given tensors.
  - Importance: necessary for allocating scratch memory before performing BitNet multiplications.

- ggml_bitnet_mul_mat_task_init(void * src1, void * qlut, void * lut_scales, void * lut_biases, int n, int k, int m, int bits)
  - Purpose: initialize per-task data for a BitNet matmul (likely builds LUTs or precomputes data dependent on src1).
  - Importance: prepares the per-task LUT data required by ggml_bitnet_mul_mat_task_compute.

- ggml_bitnet_mul_mat_task_compute(void * src0, void * scales, void * qlut, void * lut_scales, void * lut_biases, void * dst, int n, int k, int m, int bits)
  - Purpose: perform the compute phase of a BitNet matmul task using preinitialized lookup tables and scales, writing to dst.
  - Importance: core computation entry used during BitNet matrix multiplication.

- ggml_bitnet_transform_tensor(struct ggml_tensor * tensor)
  - Purpose: transform a tensor into the internal layout or metadata required by BitNet (likely populating bitnet_tensor_extra or reformatting quantized weights).
  - Importance: used when preparing models/tensors for BitNet operations.

- ggml_bitnet_get_type_bits(enum ggml_type type)
  - Purpose: return the number of bits associated with the given ggml_type (e.g., Q4_0 -> 4, IQ2_XXS -> 2, etc).
  - Importance: used to interpret quantized data layout and select appropriate routines.

- ggml_bitnet_set_n_threads(int n_threads)
  - Purpose: set the number of threads used by BitNet routines.
  - Importance: controls parallelism for performance tuning.

- ggml_qgemm_lut (ARM variant) — declared under #if defined(GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1)
  - Prototype: GGML_API void ggml_qgemm_lut(int m, int k, void* A, void* LUT, void* Scales, void* LUT_Scales, void* C);
  - Purpose: ARM TL1-specific low-level qgemm using lookup tables. Likely optimized for ARM NEON.
  - Importance: platform-specific kernel; enabled only when GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1 is defined.

- ggml_preprocessor (ARM variant) — declared under #if defined(GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1)
  - Prototype: GGML_API void ggml_preprocessor(int m, int k, void* B, void* LUT_Scales, void* QLUT);
  - Purpose: ARM TL1-specific preprocessing of B (weights) into LUT-based structures.
  - Importance: preparation step for ggml_qgemm_lut on ARM TL1.

- ggml_qgemm_lut (x86 variant) — declared under #if defined(GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2)
  - Prototype: GGML_API void ggml_qgemm_lut(int bs, int m, int k, int BK, void* A, void* sign, void* LUT, void* Scales, void* LUT_Scales, void* C);
  - Purpose: x86 TL2-specific low-level qgemm; has additional parameters (bs, BK, sign) compared to ARM variant.
  - Importance: x86-optimized kernel; different prototype indicates different internal tiling/format.

- ggml_preprocessor (x86 variant) — declared under #if defined(GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2)
  - Prototype: GGML_API void ggml_preprocessor(int bs, int m, int three_k, int two_k, void* B, void* LUT_Scales, void* Three_QLUT, void* Two_QLUT);
  - Purpose: x86 TL2-specific preprocessing; produces separate LUTs for different quantization widths (three_k, two_k).
  - Importance: platform-specific preprocessing routine used by the x86 qgemm implementation.

  - GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1 and GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2 — control inclusion of platform-specific optimized functions with different prototypes for ARM vs x86.
- There are no Clang- or GCC-specific macros (e.g., __clang__, __GNUC__, or compiler-attribute based directives) in this file, nor any mention of android/aarch64 or other platform-specific comments requesting a particular compiler. The header uses standard conditional compilation for CPU feature or target-specific implementations only.

cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.14)  # for add_link_options and implicit target directories.
project("bitnet.cpp" C CXX)
include(CheckIncludeFileCXX)

set(CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS ON)

if (NOT XCODE AND NOT MSVC AND NOT CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE)
    set(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE Release CACHE STRING "Build type" FORCE)
    set_property(CACHE CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE PROPERTY STRINGS "Debug" "Release" "MinSizeRel" "RelWithDebInfo")
endif()

set(CMAKE_RUNTIME_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/bin)

# option list
option(BITNET_ARM_TL1    "bitnet.cpp: use tl1 on arm platform"    OFF)
option(BITNET_X86_TL2    "bitnet.cpp: use tl2 on x86 platform"    OFF)


set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED true)
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED true)
set(THREADS_PREFER_PTHREAD_FLAG ON)

# override ggml options
set(GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1    ${BITNET_ARM_TL1})
set(GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2    ${BITNET_X86_TL2})

if (GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1)
    add_compile_definitions(GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1)
endif()
if (GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2)
    add_compile_definitions(GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2)
endif()

if (CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID STREQUAL "GNU" OR CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID STREQUAL "GNU")
    add_compile_options(-fpermissive)
endif()

find_package(Threads REQUIRED)

add_subdirectory(src)
add_subdirectory(3rdparty/llama.cpp)

# install

include(GNUInstallDirs)
include(CMakePackageConfigHelpers)

set(LLAMA_INCLUDE_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}
    CACHE PATH "Location of header files")
set(LLAMA_LIB_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    CACHE PATH "Location of library files")
set(LLAMA_BIN_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}
    CACHE PATH "Location of binary files")
set(LLAMA_BUILD_NUMBER ${BUILD_NUMBER})
set(LLAMA_BUILD_COMMIT ${BUILD_COMMIT})
set(LLAMA_INSTALL_VERSION 0.0.${BUILD_NUMBER})

get_target_property(GGML_DIRECTORY ggml SOURCE_DIR)
get_directory_property(GGML_DIR_DEFINES DIRECTORY ${GGML_DIRECTORY} COMPILE_DEFINITIONS)
get_target_property(GGML_TARGET_DEFINES ggml COMPILE_DEFINITIONS)
set(GGML_TRANSIENT_DEFINES ${GGML_TARGET_DEFINES} ${GGML_DIR_DEFINES})
get_target_property(GGML_LINK_LIBRARIES ggml LINK_LIBRARIES)

get_directory_property(LLAMA_TRANSIENT_DEFINES COMPILE_DEFINITIONS)

write_basic_package_version_file(
        ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/LlamaConfigVersion.cmake
    VERSION ${LLAMA_INSTALL_VERSION}
    COMPATIBILITY SameMajorVersion)

install(FILES ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/LlamaConfig.cmake
              ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/LlamaConfigVersion.cmake
        DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/Llama)

set_target_properties(llama PROPERTIES PUBLIC_HEADER ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/llama.h)
install(TARGETS llama LIBRARY PUBLIC_HEADER)

### File Overview
This CMakeLists.txt bootstraps the build for the bitnet.cpp project: it sets minimum CMake version, declares the project (project("bitnet.cpp" C CXX)), configures build-type defaults, compiler/runtime output settings, feature options, some compile-time defines, finds Threads, adds subdirectories (src and 3rdparty/llama.cpp), and writes/install configuration for the llama target.

### Architecture
- Top-level bootstrap: cmake_minimum_required and project("bitnet.cpp" C CXX).
- Global configuration: include(CheckIncludeFileCXX), toggles like CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS, build type defaulting, output directory, language standard flags, and THREADS_PREFER_PTHREAD_FLAG.
- Option declarations and propagation: BITNET_ARM_TL1, BITNET_X86_TL2 are declared; corresponding GGML_BITNET_* variables are set and translated into compile definitions.
- Compiler-specific tweak: a conditional that checks CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID and CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID and adds -fpermissive for GCC.
- Dependency discovery and composition: find_package(Threads REQUIRED), add_subdirectory(src), add_subdirectory(3rdparty/llama.cpp).
- Packaging/install: includes GNUInstallDirs and CMakePackageConfigHelpers, configures LLAMA_* install-related cache variables, queries properties from ggml and llama targets/directories, writes version file, and installs the llama target and its public header.

The main components interact by setting global variables and compile definitions early, then delegating actual target creation to subdirectories, and finally collecting properties from subprojects (ggml) to produce installable package artifacts.

### Functions & Classes
Note: presenting CMake command names exactly as they appear in this file.

- cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.14) — Declares the minimum required CMake version (3.14). Important because it enables features like add_link_options and implicit target directories referenced in the comment.

- project("bitnet.cpp" C CXX) — Declares the project name and enabled languages (C and CXX). This call initializes project-level variables (including CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID and CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID) and influences how and when compiler-related variables may be set. Critical: moving or delaying this affects compiler detection semantics.

- include(CheckIncludeFileCXX) — Includes the CheckIncludeFileCXX module. Used for subsequent include checks in subdirectories or later configuration.

- set(CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS ON) — Enables generation of compile_commands.json. Global setting for tooling.

- if (NOT XCODE AND NOT MSVC AND NOT CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE)
    - set(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE Release CACHE STRING "Build type" FORCE)
    - set_property(CACHE CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE PROPERTY STRINGS "Debug" "Release" "MinSizeRel" "RelWithDebInfo")
  endif()
  — This block checks if no generator-specific multi-config environment (XCODE or MSVC) and CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE is unset; if so, forces CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE to Release in the cache and sets allowed values. Important for defaulting build type in single-config generators.

- set(CMAKE_RUNTIME_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/bin) — Sets runtime output directory for built binaries.

- option(BITNET_ARM_TL1    "bitnet.cpp: use tl1 on arm platform"    OFF) — Declares the BITNET_ARM_TL1 option with description and default OFF. Used to control ARM-specific behavior.

- option(BITNET_X86_TL2    "bitnet.cpp: use tl2 on x86 platform"    OFF) — Declares the BITNET_X86_TL2 option. Controls x86-specific behavior.

- set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED true) — Ensures a C++ standard is required (no specific standard number set here in this file).

- set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11) — Sets the C standard to C11.

- set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED true) — Requires C standard to be enforced.

- set(THREADS_PREFER_PTHREAD_FLAG ON) — Hint for Threads package to prefer pthreads on certain platforms.

- set(GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1    ${BITNET_ARM_TL1}) — Propagates BITNET_ARM_TL1 into GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1 variable for the ggml subproject.

- set(GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2    ${BITNET_X86_TL2}) — Propagates BITNET_X86_TL2 into GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2 for ggml.

- if (GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1)
    add_compile_definitions(GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1)
  endif()
  — If GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1 is set, add a preprocessor definition GGML_BITNET_ARM_TL1 globally. Important to enable ARM-specific code paths in ggml.

- if (GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2)
    add_compile_definitions(GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2)
  endif()
  — If GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2 is set, add a preprocessor definition GGML_BITNET_X86_TL2 globally. Enables x86-specific code paths in ggml.

- if (CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID STREQUAL "GNU" OR CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID STREQUAL "GNU")
    add_compile_options(-fpermissive)
  endif()
  — Compiler-detection conditional that checks CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID and CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID and adds the -fpermissive compile option when using GNU compilers. This is a key block that depends on compiler-identifying variables, which are typically populated by project() (or by toolchain/cache directives provided before project()).

- find_package(Threads REQUIRED) — Finds and configures the Threads package (pthread etc). Required for linking.

- add_subdirectory(src) — Adds the src subdirectory; expected to define the main targets for the project.

- add_subdirectory(3rdparty/llama.cpp) — Adds the 3rdparty/llama.cpp subdirectory, which provides ggml and/or llama targets used later.

- include(GNUInstallDirs) — Includes the GNUInstallDirs module to obtain conventional install directories (CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR, CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR, CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR, etc).

- include(CMakePackageConfigHelpers) — Includes helper functions for writing package config/version files, e.g., write_basic_package_version_file.

- set(LLAMA_INCLUDE_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}
    CACHE PATH "Location of header files") — Sets a CACHE variable for header install location.

- set(LLAMA_LIB_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    CACHE PATH "Location of library files") — Sets a CACHE variable for library install location.

- set(LLAMA_BIN_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}
    CACHE PATH "Location of binary files") — Sets a CACHE variable for binary install location.

- set(LLAMA_BUILD_NUMBER ${BUILD_NUMBER}) — Sets LLAMA_BUILD_NUMBER to BUILD_NUMBER (depends on external variables passed in).

- set(LLAMA_BUILD_COMMIT ${BUILD_COMMIT}) — Sets LLAMA_BUILD_COMMIT to BUILD_COMMIT.

- set(LLAMA_INSTALL_VERSION 0.0.${BUILD_NUMBER}) — Constructs an install version string using BUILD_NUMBER.

- get_target_property(GGML_DIRECTORY ggml SOURCE_DIR) — Queries the ggml target for SOURCE_DIR and stores it in GGML_DIRECTORY. Used to inspect ggml's directory-level properties.

- get_directory_property(GGML_DIR_DEFINES DIRECTORY ${GGML_DIRECTORY} COMPILE_DEFINITIONS) — Gets directory-level COMPILE_DEFINITIONS from the ggml directory and stores them in GGML_DIR_DEFINES.

- get_target_property(GGML_TARGET_DEFINES ggml COMPILE_DEFINITIONS) — Gets the target-level COMPILE_DEFINITIONS from target ggml into GGML_TARGET_DEFINES.

- set(GGML_TRANSIENT_DEFINES ${GGML_TARGET_DEFINES} ${GGML_DIR_DEFINES}) — Aggregates ggml target and directory compile definitions into GGML_TRANSIENT_DEFINES for use when packaging.

- get_target_property(GGML_LINK_LIBRARIES ggml LINK_LIBRARIES) — Retrieves ggml target link libraries into GGML_LINK_LIBRARIES.

- get_directory_property(LLAMA_TRANSIENT_DEFINES COMPILE_DEFINITIONS) — Retrieves current directory COMPILE_DEFINITIONS into LLAMA_TRANSIENT_DEFINES.

- write_basic_package_version_file(
        ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/LlamaConfigVersion.cmake
    VERSION ${LLAMA_INSTALL_VERSION}
    COMPATIBILITY SameMajorVersion)
  — Writes a LlamaConfigVersion.cmake file for package versioning using CMakePackageConfigHelpers. Important for downstream find_package(Llama) usage.

- install(FILES ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/LlamaConfig.cmake
              ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/LlamaConfigVersion.cmake
        DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/Llama)
  — Installs the generated package config files to the cmake folder in the install lib dir.

- set_target_properties(llama PROPERTIES PUBLIC_HEADER ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/llama.h) — Declares the llama public header (for installing headers) on the llama target.

- install(TARGETS llama LIBRARY PUBLIC_HEADER) — Installs the llama target (library) and its public header(s).

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
The agent's goal is to "Get exact content to reference where to insert compiler forcing logic before project() or suggest moving project()". Relevant points from this file:

output.pr_title: Fix build error with GCC by forcing Clang in CMake on android/aarch64
output.pr_body:
GCC does not recognize Clang-specific warning flags like -Wunreachable-code-break and -Wunreachable-code-return, which are passed by upstream submodules (e.g., ggml). This patch forces CMake to use Clang via command-line arguments, avoiding the need to patch nested submodules.

This resolves compiler errors without modifying submodule source code.
output.masca_optimized:
Goal: Ensure CMake uses Clang (clang/clang++) automatically for Android aarch64 builds so Clang-only flags passed by ggml and other submodules do not break compilation when the system's default compiler is GCC.

Key findings from project exploration:
- The top-level CMakeLists.txt declares project("bitnet.cpp" C CXX) immediately after cmake_minimum_required. CMake determines compilers when project() runs. Therefore any change that forces which compiler to use must be applied before project() is called (or be passed via the real command line to the top-level CMake invocation).
- There is already a compiler-dependent block later in top-level CMakeLists.txt that checks CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID / CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID to add -fpermissive for GCC. That block relies on CMake having detected the compilers and thus runs after project().
- The src/CMakeLists.txt contains a gate that emits a fatal error if both C and C++ compilers are not Clang or GCC. Forcing Clang will make this gate satisfied.

Recommended change (minimal, focused):
- Modify top-level CMakeLists.txt by inserting a small pre-project detection-and-force block immediately before project("bitnet.cpp" C CXX). That block should detect Android/aarch64 and, unless a user explicitly set a compiler, set the CMAKE_C_COMPILER and CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER cache variables to clang and clang++ (using CACHE FILEPATH ... FORCE). The code should avoid overriding an explicit user-provided compiler.

Why this is the right place:
- CMake determines the compiler at project() time. Setting cache variables before project() ensures the requested compiler is used project-wide (including in nested add_subdirectory calls, submodules, and ggml), so Clang-only flags from submodules will be accepted.

Safety notes and behavior:
- Do not override compilers if the user already set CMAKE_C_COMPILER/CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER on the command line; only set them when not provided.
- Restrict forcing to Android and aarch64 (CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME == "Android" and CMAKE_SYSTEM_PROCESSOR matches aarch64/arm64) to avoid surprising behavior on other platforms.
- Use CACHE FILEPATH to set compiler paths to names "clang" and "clang++" so CMake will search PATH; an alternative is to accept paths via environment or explicit -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER arguments if desired by the caller.

Example insertion (plain text; to be added before project() in top-level CMakeLists.txt):

if (NOT DEFINED CMAKE_C_COMPILER OR CMAKE_C_COMPILER STREQUAL "")
  if (CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME STREQUAL "Android" AND (CMAKE_SYSTEM_PROCESSOR MATCHES "aarch64" OR CMAKE_SYSTEM_PROCESSOR MATCHES "arm64"))
    # Force clang/clang++ on Android aarch64 to avoid GCC failing on Clang-specific flags from submodules
    set(CMAKE_C_COMPILER "clang" CACHE FILEPATH "C compiler" FORCE)
    set(CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER "clang++" CACHE FILEPATH "C++ compiler" FORCE)
  endif()
endif()

(Place this just after cmake_minimum_required(...), before project(...)).

No changes to ggml submodule sources are required; the patch is local to top-level CMake configuration.

Files to review/test after change:
- Ensure the new logic runs in your cross-build setup (sometimes toolchain files or -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE set CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME early). Confirm clang is resolvable on PATH or provide full path.
- Test both the Android aarch64 case and non-Android cases to ensure nothing else changes.

This is a minimal, robust fix aligned with the PR goal (force Clang on android/aarch64 via CMake) and avoids touching nested submodules.

agents.name: context_planner_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",
  "function_to_modify": "Animation.time_spanned_alpha",
  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",
  "side_effects": "May require downstream animations to call this method"
}
```

### For non-Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",
  "file_to_modify": "config/routes.yaml",
  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```
